sourceMovie.title: The Presidio
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Presidio (1988) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Peter Hyams's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The International
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Presidio clicked for you, The International (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Primal Fear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Presidio clicked for you, Primal Fear (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Presidio?
faq.answer: Try The International, Primal Fear, 16 Blocks—each shares DNA with The Presidio while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Presidio?
faq.answer: The Presidio (1988) is best described as Action, Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Presidio worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
